classes.reasoning: Document is titled 'STUDY ABSTRACT' and provides a high-level summary of the VIRTUOSE trial protocol, including objectives, methodology, inclusion/exclusion criteria, treatment strategies, and expected results. This is NOT the full protocol but a synopsis document.

sponsor_name: CHU de Rennes (Rennes University Hospital Centre)
trial_title: Efficiency of sildenafil on the absolute claudication distance of peripheral arterial disease patients with intermittent claudication. A phase III, National, Multicenter, Prospective, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled trial.
indication: Peripheral Arterial Disease (PAD) with intermittent claudication
summary: This is a study abstract (synopsis) for the VIRTUOSE trial, a phase III randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study investigating the efficacy of sildenafil (140 mg/day) versus placebo on walking capacity in patients with peripheral arterial disease and intermittent claudication. The primary objective is to determine whether sildenafil improves absolute claudication distance at week 24, with follow-up extending to week 48 and secondary outcomes including revascularization rates, quality of life, and cardiovascular events.
